title: 10 best herbal creams for altitude sickness

remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba extract in these creams has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, which can help alleviate symptoms of high altitude sickness such as headaches and dizziness.

Huperzine A: Huperzine A, a compound derived from Maidenhair Tree leaves, is known for its ability to increase oxygenation of tissues and reduce inflammation, both of which can help mitigate the effects of high altitude sickness.

Saponins: Saponins present in Maidenhair Tree creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling, pain, and oxidative stress associated with high altitude sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with altitude sickness because it increases blood flow to the brain and extremities, reducing the effects of low oxygen levels. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, bilobalide and ginkgolides, improve circulation and oxygen delivery to tissues, making it easier to adapt to high altitudes. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may help reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function, further alleviating the symptoms associated with altitude sickness. This makes it a popular natural remedy for travelers and adventurers.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): Assist in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation at high altitudes, which can help alleviate symptoms such as shortness of breath and dizziness.

Ginkgolides (specifically Ginkgolide A and B): Help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the lungs, which can improve oxygenation and reduce the severity of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their high bilobalide content, a compound that may help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation associated with altitude sickness.
(Optional) Barks: Barks are sometimes used due to their potential antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate oxidative stress associated with high altitude exposure.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 ounces of coconut oil and 0.5 ounces of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 120 to 140 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in 1 teaspoon of ginkgo biloba extract powder.
Add 2 drops of vitamin e oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Golden root cream helps reduce inflammation caused by high-altitude exposure, which can lead to symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in golden root cream help protect against oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, reducing the risk of cell damage and tissue injury.

Cardiovascular support: Golden root's vasodilatory effects help improve blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, which can alleviate symptoms like headaches, dizziness, and nausea associated with altitude sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea, also known as golden root, creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain adaptogenic properties that aid the body in adapting to high altitudes. These creams typically include Rhodiola rosea extracts which increase oxygen utilization, reduce fatigue, and improve mental clarity. By enhancing the body's ability to adapt, Rhodiola rosea creams can help alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness such as headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath. This natural remedy has been used by mountaineers and adventurers to support their high-altitude endeavors.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: Rosavin is a key constituent in Rhodiola rosea that helps alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by improving cardiovascular function and reducing oxidative stress caused by high altitude.

Salidroside: Salidroside is a phenylethylene glycoside in Rhodiola rosea that helps mitigate altitude sickness symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving mitochondrial function, and enhancing antioxidant defenses.

Tyrosol: Tyrosol, a phenolic compound in Rhodiola rosea, may help alleviate altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular function, and enhancing the body's ability to adapt to high-altitude conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Rhodiola rosea are rich in bioactive compounds, making them a primary source for extracting the plant's therapeutic properties.
Leaves: Rhodiola rosea leaves are used due to their high content of adaptogenic compounds, which help the body adapt to high-altitude conditions.
Barks: Rhodiola rosea barks are employed for their rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, contributing to the plant's potential benefits for alleviating altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of rhodiola rosea roots and clean them thoroughly with water to remove any dirt.
Dry the cleaned rhodiola rosea roots in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried rhodiola rosea roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 1 teaspoon of rhodiola rosea powder with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10-15 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng's antioxidant compounds help protect cells from damage caused by high-altitude conditions, such as oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate symptoms like headaches and fatigue.

Vasodilatory properties: Ginseng's ability to dilate blood vessels can improve circulation and increase oxygen delivery to tissues, helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as shortness of breath and dizziness.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng is believed to help the body adapt to high-altitude stress by reducing cortisol levels, improving sleep quality, and boosting energy levels, which can help alleviate symptoms like insomnia and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with altitude sickness because of its adaptogenic properties. It enables the body to adapt to high-altitude stress by increasing oxygen supply and reducing fatigue. The cream's active compounds, such as ginsenosides, help to improve cardiovascular function and boost the immune system. By supporting the body's natural response to altitude stress, Panax ginseng creams can alleviate symptoms like headaches, dizziness, and nausea, making it a valuable remedy for individuals traveling to high-altitude regions.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpene saponins have been shown to improve cardiovascular function and increase oxygen delivery to tissues, which can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
Ginsenoside Rg1: This specific ginsenoside has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce oxidative stress associated with high altitudes.
Panaxynol: This lignan has been reported to have vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood flow and reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng contain ginsenosides, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by improving blood circulation and reducing oxidative stress.
Leaves: The leaves of Panax ginseng are also rich in ginsenosides, which contribute to their potential benefits in treating altitude sickness.
Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng contain bioactive compounds that may help in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, both of which are relevant to altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of dried panax ginseng root and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ginseng powder with 50g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Melt the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until the wax has fully dissolved.
Add 20g of distilled water and 5g of vegetable glycerin to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain caused by altitude-induced inflammation in the lungs, brain, and other organs.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of the cream can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate symptoms of altitude sickness such as fatigue, headaches, and shortness of breath.

Immune-boosting: The herbal ingredients in the cream may stimulate the immune system to respond more effectively to high-altitude stressors, reducing the severity and duration of altitude sickness symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with altitude sickness because of its adaptogenic properties that help the body adjust to high-altitude conditions. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting oxygenation in the body. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress caused by high altitude, supporting overall health and well-being during mountainous travel or high-altitude activities. This natural remedy may provide relief from altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: This flavonoid glycoside has been shown to increase nitric oxide production, which can help improve blood flow and reduce the risk of altitude sickness.
Cichoric acid: As an anti-inflammatory compound, cichoric acid may help mitigate the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with high-altitude environments.

Alkylphenols: These phenolic compounds may contribute to the antioxidant properties of Echinacea purpurea, helping to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative damage caused by high-altitude conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant properties to help reduce oxidative stress caused by high altitudes.
Flowers: They are used for their immunomodulatory properties to help boost the immune system and combat altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 ounce of dried echinacea purpurea flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids then mix it with 1 ounce of beeswax.
Melt the beeswax and echinacea mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and refrigerate until it thickens into a cream.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Garlic's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress caused by high altitude conditions, which can contribute to symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in garlic cream may help alleviate inflammation and swelling in the lungs and brain, common side effects of altitude sickness.

Cardiovascular protection: Garlic's ability to lower blood pressure and improve blood flow may help reduce the risk of cardiovascular complications associated with high-altitude environments, such as heart problems and cerebral edema.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, creams helps with altitude sickness because of its natural vasodilatory properties. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin, help to widen blood vessels and improve circulation, which can aid in the body's ability to adapt to high altitudes. Additionally, garlic creams may help to reduce inflammation and promote the production of red blood cells, further alleviating symptoms associated with altitude sickness. This can lead to improved oxygenation and reduced fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: A sulfur compound responsible for the distinct odor and flavor of garlic, allicin is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It may help alleviate altitude sickness by reducing inflammation in the body and protecting against oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions.

S-allylcysteine (SAC): A bioactive compound derived from allicin, SAC has shown potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. It may help mitigate the effects of altitude sickness by protecting the body from oxidative damage and reducing inflammation in the affected areas.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, quercetin may help alleviate altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Its ability to scavenge free radicals and protect against cellular damage may contribute to its potential benefits in high-altitude conditions.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of allicin, a compound that helps to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness.
Roots: The roots are used for their medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried allium sativum roots, 50 grams of beeswax, 50 grams of coconut oil, and 20 grams of shea butter.
Infuse 100 grams of the allium sativum roots in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for four to six weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 200 grams of coconut oil, 100 grams of shea butter, and 50 grams of beeswax.
Melt the combined mixture in a double boiler and stir occasionally for 30 minutes to 40 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and harden for at least two hours before applying to the skin.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like parthenolide, which can help reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and joint pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Feverfew's parthenolide also has vasodilatory properties, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the risk of altitude sickness caused by inadequate oxygen delivery to the brain and other organs.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew cream contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals formed during oxidative stress at high altitudes, protecting against tissue damage and inflammation caused by hypoxia (oxygen deficiency).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tanacetum parthenium, also known as feverfew, creams helps with altitude sickness because of its unique properties that aid in reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation. The active compounds in feverfew, such as parthenolide, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness, including headaches and fatigue. Additionally, feverfew's ability to improve blood flow can help the body adapt to high altitudes, making it a valuable remedy for those who venture to mountainous regions.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with altitude sickness.
Parthenolide: This sesquiterpene lactone has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help manage the headaches and fatigue commonly experienced at high altitudes.

Fatty acids (e.g., oleic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their bioactive compounds, including parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Flowers: Used due to their high content of parthenolide and other bioactive compounds that may help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Buds: Used due to their bioactive compounds, including parthenolide, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate headaches associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of tanacetum parthenium flowers in their blooming stage when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to achieve a moisture content of 10%.
Grind 50 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Stir the mixture for 15 minutes and remove from heat to allow it to cool and solidify into a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a compound found in white willow bark, has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and joint pain commonly associated with altitude sickness.

Menthol: Menthol, another key ingredient in herbal white willow creams, helps to ease congestion and respiratory issues caused by high altitudes, making it easier for the body to adapt to changes in air pressure.

Glycosides: The glycosides present in white willow bark, such as salicin, have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to extremities, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness like swelling, joint pain, and fatigue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salix alba, also known as white willow, creams helps with altitude sickness because it contains salicin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When hikers ascend to high altitudes, their bodies experience a decrease in oxygen levels, leading to inflammation and discomfort. Salicin in Salix alba creams can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and joint pain, making it a useful remedy for altitude sickness. Its natural properties also promote relaxation and reduce stress, further aiding in recovery.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin, a phenolic compound, helps with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and relieving headaches and body aches associated with high altitude.
Salicylic acid: Salicylic acid, a phenolic acid, helps by acting as a vasodilator, improving blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, which is often impaired at high altitudes.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid, helps by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high altitude, which can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make creams for altitude sickness due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Barks: Used to make creams for altitude sickness due to their potential vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects.
Roots: Used to make creams for altitude sickness due to their potential adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried salix alba bark, carefully selecting branches with no signs of decay or contamination.
Combine the dried bark with 500ml of carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil, in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 4-6 hours, stirring occasionally, to facilitate infusion and extraction.
Strain the oil mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, collecting the infused oil in a clean container.
Mix the infused oil with 10% beeswax and 5% vitamin e oil, heating the mixture until the wax melts and cools to form a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn's cardiac glycosides help to reduce blood pressure and increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headache and shortness of breath.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in hawthorn creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the body caused by high-altitude exposure, and also protect against oxidative stress.

Proanthocyanidins: Proanthocyanidins are powerful antioxidants that can help to protect the blood vessels from damage caused by high altitude, reducing the risk of edema and other circulatory problems associated with acute mountain sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with altitude sickness because it contains flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins that aid in improving cardiovascular function and blood flow to the brain. The increased oxygen delivery to the brain helps alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue. Additionally, hawthorn's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress caused by high altitude, making it a potential natural remedy to help acclimatize to high elevations and reduce the severity of altitude sickness.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with altitude sickness.
Catechins: A type of phenolic compound that may help improve blood flow and reduce the risk of high-altitude pulmonary edema (HAPE) by promoting vasodilation and improving cardiovascular function.
Tannins: A type of polyphenol that may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue, by reducing inflammation and improving antioxidant defenses.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Barks: The bark's flavonoids and saponins help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits of Crataegus monogyna contain flavonoids and other antioxidants that help improve cardiovascular function and reduce altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried fruit from the tree for use in the herbal cream.
Infuse the dried fruit in 100ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add 20g of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Pour the hot mixture into small containers and allow it to cool completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and discomfort in the body caused by high altitude, making it easier to breathe and move around.

Vasodilation: The capsaicin present in ginger helps to dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen flow to the brain and other organs, which can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as dizziness and lightheadedness.

Digestive aid: Ginger's carminative and anti-spasmodic properties help soothe digestive issues that often accompany high-altitude travel, such as nausea, cramping, and bloating, allowing the body to focus on adapting to the new environment.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with altitude sickness because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to aid digestion. At high altitudes, the body experiences decreased oxygen levels, which can lead to nausea, headaches, and fatigue. Ginger's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, helping the body adapt to the changed environment. Additionally, ginger has been shown to ease nausea and digestive discomfort commonly associated with altitude sickness. This makes it a valuable remedy for travelers and climbers.
remedy_constituents: Shogaols: These gingerol derivatives have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the fluid buildup in the lungs that can occur with altitude sickness (High-Altitude Pulmonary Edema, HAPE).

Gingerols: Specifically, 6-gingerol, a major compound in ginger, has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which can help improve blood circulation and reduce the risk of High-Altitude Cerebral Edema (HACE) by improving blood flow to the brain.

Curcuminoids (a subset of phenolic compounds): While not exclusive to ginger, curcuminoids have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used for their rich content of gingerols and shogaols, which can help reduce nausea and other symptoms associated with high altitudes.
Buds: The buds are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which can aid in reducing inflammation and alleviating altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with clean gardening shears to avoid contamination and preserve potency.
Wash the harvested rhizomes thoroughly with lukewarm water to remove dirt and debris for 5 minutes.
Grate the washed rhizomes using a fine grater to release the active compounds for 10 minutes.
Mix the grated zingiber officinale with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler to melt.
Whip the cooled mixture using an electric mixer until it becomes smooth and creamy for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: Valerian's sedative and anti-anxiety properties can help alleviate the anxiety and stress often associated with altitude sickness, allowing individuals to better cope with the physical symptoms.

Pain relief: Valerian has been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the pain and discomfort caused by high-altitude conditions such as headaches, fatigue, and joint pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Valerian's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve circulation, reducing the risk of hypoxia (oxygen deficiency) and other complications associated with altitude sickness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with altitude sickness because it contains valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect on the body. At high altitudes, the body experiences increased stress due to lower oxygen levels. The valerenic acid in Valeriana officinalis creams helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress caused by altitude sickness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate headaches and fatigue often associated with altitude sickness, making it a helpful remedy for those experiencing these symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This is a sesquiterpene that acts as a GABA receptor modulator, which may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as anxiety and insomnia by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Valerenal: This is a sesquiterpene with sedative properties, which may help mitigate the effects of high altitude on the body by promoting sleep and reducing inflammation.

Valeranone: This is a sesquiterpene that has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress caused by high altitude, reducing the risk of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root contains valerenic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness.
Roots: Valerian root also contains valerenol, which has sedative properties that can help to calm the body and mind during high-altitude conditions.
Roots: The sesquiterpenes present in valerian roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in relieving symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Extract the valeriana officinalis roots by macerating 20 grams in 100 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to separate the plant material.
Combine the extracted oil with beeswax and shea butter in a 1:1:1 ratio by weight.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with altitude sickness because of its ability to promote oxygen flow and reduce inflammation in the body. The cream's active ingredients, including hypericin and hyperforin, work together to increase blood circulation, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea that occur at high elevations. Additionally, St John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the lungs, a common complication of altitude sickness, allowing for easier breathing and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by high altitudes.

Hypotensive properties: St John's Wort's ability to lower blood pressure can help alleviate symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS), such as headaches and fatigue, by reducing the strain on the cardiovascular system at high elevations.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory flavonoids: These compounds may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by hypobaric hypoxia (high altitude) and reduce the risk of chronic mountain sickness (CMS).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as hyperforin and adhyperforin, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Buds: Containing a high concentration of essential oils, including limonene and beta-caryophyllene, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the dried plant material with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a heat-proof glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for two hours to create an infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a coffee filter into another heat-proof container and discard the solids.
Mix the filtered oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil according to a ratio of 1:0.5:0.2 and heat until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain soothing properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and insomnia. Lavender's calming essence can also aid in reducing stress and anxiety, common afflictions of those affected by high elevations. Additionally, the cream's natural cooling sensation can ease the discomfort of dry skin often associated with dry mountain air, allowing individuals to better acclimate to their new environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce swelling and pain caused by acute mountain sickness (AMS).
Calming and anxiolytic effects: The calming properties of lavender can help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with high-altitude travel, which is a common contributor to AMS symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant compounds may help protect the body against oxidative stress caused by increased oxygen levels at high altitudes, which can contribute to AMS development.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with acute mountain sickness (AMS).
Buds: The buds of the English lavender plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have vasodilatory effects, helping to improve blood flow and oxygenation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves and flowers from a reliable source.
Dry the plant material using air circulation at room temperature for 7 days.
Combine 20% dried lavandula angustifolia, 30% sweet almond oil, and 50% coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 60°c for 30 minutes under gentle stirring to infuse the herbs into the oils.
Filter and bottle the infused cream after cooling to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with altitude sickness because they provide a natural relief from symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea. The menthol in peppermint oil is able to thin out mucus in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing at high elevations. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the brain and body, alleviating pressure headaches caused by altitude changes. As a result, herbal peppermint creams offer a soothing and effective way to manage the discomforts of altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, alleviating symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS) such as coughing and shortness of breath.
Menthol: Stimulates circulation and improves oxygenation of the body's tissues, which can help mitigate altitude-related fatigue, headaches, and dizziness.
Camphor: Has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving symptoms such as headache, nausea, and vomiting associated with high-altitude travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain compounds like menthol and methyl salicylate, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and nausea.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is a specialized underground stem that contains high concentrations of menthone and other active compounds, making it an effective ingredient in creams for relieving acute mountain sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a well-established plant in the morning.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator at 55 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground peppermint powder with a cream base consisting of 50% beeswax, 30% coconut oil, and 20% shea butter.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and label with date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain the herb Achillea millefolium, which has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of high altitude illnesses. The cream's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the lungs and brain caused by decreased oxygen levels. Additionally, yarrow's expectorant properties can help ease coughing fits associated with mountain sickness. By reducing inflammation and congestion, herbal yarrow creams provide relief from the discomfort and shortness of breath often experienced at high elevations.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow creams contain compounds like achillein and sesquiterpene lactones, which reduce inflammation in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and joint pain.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in yarrow cream help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, which can exacerbate symptoms of altitude sickness.

Vasodilatory properties: Yarrow's natural vasodilators, like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help to dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen delivery to tissues and organs, which is particularly important at high altitudes where oxygen levels are lower.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to soothe and calm inflammation, which can occur as a result of high-altitude exposure.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that help reduce swelling and promote blood flow, making them effective in treating altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves in morning dew for optimal potency and store them in an airtight container immediately.
Combine 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves with 100 milliliters of olive oil and infuse the mixture at room temperature for 4 hours.
Filter the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and discard the residue after 30 minutes.
Mix the filtered oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain natural ingredients that work together to combat the physical stress of high elevations. The anti-inflammatory properties of elderflowers and chamomile soothe the body's response to decreased oxygen levels, reducing swelling and pain in the hands and feet. Meanwhile, the antioxidants in green tea and ginkgo biloba help protect against oxidative damage caused by altitude-induced inflammation, promoting overall respiratory health and reducing symptoms of acute mountain sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high altitude exposure, which can exacerbate symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS).

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, may help alleviate headache, nausea, and fatigue associated with AMS by reducing swelling and pain in the body.

Vasodilatory properties: The active compounds in elderberry, particularly quercetin, may help dilate blood vessels, which can improve oxygen delivery to the brain and other organs at high altitudes, potentially reducing symptoms of AMS.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.
Barks: Containing triterpenes and tannins, these compounds help soothe and protect the skin while reducing oxidative stress caused by high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Combine 100g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 200ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for infusion and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter to create a creamy texture then store it at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with altitude sickness because its mucilaginous properties soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by high altitudes. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to alleviate congestion, coughing, and shortness of breath, common symptoms of altitude sickness. Additionally, slippery elm's demulcent properties create a protective barrier in the throat and lungs, allowing for easier breathing and relaxation, making it an effective natural remedy for combating the discomforts associated with high-altitude travel.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm cream contains mucilages, which are protective and soothing agents that can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the lungs and airways caused by altitude sickness.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in slippery elm cream can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, which can contribute to the development of altitude sickness.

Demulcents: Slippery elm's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to soothe and calm irritated airways and lungs, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a protective substance that helps soothe and calm irritated skin caused by high altitude conditions.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids which have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried ulmus rubra bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powder with 50g of beeswax and 30g of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating them until the wax melts completely after 20 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 15ml of lavender essential oil, stirring well to combine for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, shaking well before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain herbal extracts that promote healthy breathing and lung function. The soothing properties of marshmallows, such as slippery elm and licorice root, help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier for people to breathe at high elevations. Additionally, marshmallow creams often include herbs like ginger and peppermint, which can help to alleviate nausea and fatigue associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as mucilage and flavonoids, can help reduce swelling in the lungs and brain caused by high-altitude hypoxia, alleviating symptoms like headaches and dizziness.

Mucilages: The thick, protective coating of mucilages in marshmallow cream can soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by altitude sickness.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow cream's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help neutralize free radicals generated by high-altitude exposure, which can damage lung tissue and worsen altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective coating that helps soothe and calm irritated skin affected by high altitudes.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and discomfort caused by altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried marshmallow root and 1 cup of dried peppermint leaves for infusion.
Steep the marshmallow root in boiling water for 10 minutes then let it cool down slowly.
Mix 250ml of coconut oil with 150ml of infused marshmallow liquid and stir well for 5 minutes.
Add 20 drops of essential oil of peppermint and 10 grams of beeswax to the mixture then heat gently until melted.
Pour the creamy mixture into small containers and let it cool down completely before application within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain natural diuretics that help reduce fluid buildup in the body, a common issue at high elevations. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also alleviate symptoms such as headaches and joint pain caused by decreased oxygen levels. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant richness helps protect against oxidative stress and damage to cells caused by altitude-induced hypoxia. By addressing these underlying physiological changes, herbal dandelion creams can provide relief from the discomforts of altitude sickness, allowing individuals to adapt more easily to high-altitude environments.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion cream's diuretic properties help to reduce fluid buildup in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as swelling and water retention.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion cream may help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with acute mountain sickness (AMS), making it easier for the body to adjust to high altitudes.

Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion cream's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by altitude changes, which can contribute to symptoms of AMS such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps maintain gut health and can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as nausea and diarrhea.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with high-altitude conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in amounts of 2 tablespoons per 8 ounces of carrier oil.
Combine the herb mixture with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to infuse the oils.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to help alleviate symptoms of high altitude exposure. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling in the brain and body caused by increased oxygen levels, while its antioxidant properties can combat free radicals that contribute to altitude-related fatigue and weakness. Additionally, licorice creams can help regulate blood flow and pressure, which is disrupted at high elevations, further reducing symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and joint pain.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice creams often contain antioxidants like flavonoids and polyphenols, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by high altitudes, thus mitigating damage to cells and tissues.

Glycyrrhetinic acid: This compound has been shown to have a direct vasodilatory effect on blood vessels, helping to increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues and organs, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and improve oxygenation in the body.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that aid in reducing blood pressure and improving circulation at high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of high-quality beeswax pellets and melt in a double boiler.
Combine 100 milliliters of distilled water with 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir 50 grams of sweet almond oil into the melted beeswax until fully incorporated.
Add 10 drops of peppermint essential oil and stir to combine before pouring into molds.
Allow the mixture to cool and set at room temperature for at least 30 minutes before packaging.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for altitude sickness are topical remedies containing plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of high-altitude conditions, such as headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath. The best herbal creams for altitude sickness include Ginkgo biloba, Astragalus membranaceus, Zingiber officinale, Panax ginseng, Rhodiola rosea, Cinchona officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Withania somnifera, and Ephedra sinica. If left untreated, altitude sickness can lead to serious complications, such as acute mountain sickness, high-altitude pulmonary edema, and even death, severely impacting the quality of life for travelers and climbers.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to altitude sickness that are treated with herbal creams are inflammation, pain, and swelling associated with conditions such as arthritis, sprains, and strains. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, calendula, and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce discomfort and promote healing. These creams can also be used to treat skin irritations and minor injuries, providing a natural alternative to conventional pain relievers.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with altitude sickness is a blend of arnica, ginger, and cayenne pepper. Arnica cream reduces inflammation and relieves pain, while ginger cream eases nausea and digestive discomfort. Cayenne pepper cream increases blood flow and helps the body acclimate to high altitudes. Applying these creams topically can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness. However, it's essential to note that these creams are not a substitute for medical treatment, and consulting a doctor is still necessary for severe cases.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with altitude sickness because they improve blood flow to the brain and other vital organs. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba help to thin the blood and reduce inflammation, which can be beneficial for people experiencing high-altitude conditions. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to enhance oxygen delivery and reduce oxidative stress can help alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue. This makes it a popular choice among travelers and adventurers.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with altitude sickness by improving blood flow to the brain and reducing oxidative stress caused by high altitude.
Bilobalide: It helps to reduce the inflammation and damage to brain cells caused by high altitude, leading to improved cognitive function and reduced risk of altitude sickness.
Ginkgolides: They help to reduce the constriction of blood vessels at high altitude, improving blood flow to the brain and reducing the risk of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree are the most used part for altitude sickness creams because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used in creams for altitude sickness due to their rich antioxidant properties, which help protect cells from damage caused by high altitude conditions.
Leaves: (second item) The leaves of Ginkgo biloba also contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, which are effective in reducing the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Extract 240ml of ginkgo biloba leaves using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio for 2 weeks.
Filter the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the plant material and obtain a clear extract.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to create a wax-oil mixture.
Gradually add 10ml of the ginkgo biloba extract to the wax-oil mixture while continuously stirring to prevent separation.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let them cool and solidify for 30-60 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain adaptogenic properties that help the body acclimate to high altitudes. The herb, also known as Huang Qi, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance the body's natural response to stress and increase oxygen delivery to cells. By promoting circulation and improving oxygenation, Astragalus membranaceus creams may help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as fatigue, headaches, and nausea, allowing individuals to ascend higher with greater comfort and safety.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain and other organs.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Astragalus membranaceus have been shown to enhance the body's natural response to high altitudes by stimulating the production of erythropoietin, which helps increase red blood cell production.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in this herb help mitigate altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, thus protecting the body from the damage caused by high-altitude conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are often used due to their high concentration of astragalosides, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Astragalus membranaceus are also used as they contain a rich combination of bioactive compounds that may help combat altitude sickness.
Leaves: The leaves are used in some formulations due to their content of flavonoids and other compounds that may aid in reducing the severity of altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried astragalus membranaceus roots from a trusted supplier or foraged sustainably in the wild.
Combine the dried roots with 500ml of boiling water in a saucepan to create a root infusion.
Steep the root infusion for 30-60 minutes to allow the bioactive compounds to release.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the resulting liquid with a base of 20 grams of beeswax and 50ml of jojoba oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with altitude sickness because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with high altitudes. The ginger compound, gingerol, has natural vasoconstrictive properties that help to reduce blood vessel dilation and alleviate headaches caused by altitude changes. Additionally, ginger's ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea can be beneficial for individuals experiencing altitude sickness. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort for travelers and adventurers.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the body caused by high altitude, thereby alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness.
Gingerdiones: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by high altitude, thereby reducing the risk of altitude sickness.

Volatile oils (specifically, gingerols' sesquiterpene and sesquiterpenoids): These compounds have vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow to the brain and other organs, thereby reducing the risk of altitude sickness and its associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high gingerol and shogaol content, which can help alleviate nausea and other symptoms associated with altitude sickness.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and improve oxygen delivery to the body, making them a useful ingredient for altitude sickness creams.

Stems: The stems of Zingiber officinale are also used in some creams due to their ability to promote blood flow and reduce blood pressure, which can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and dizziness.
remedy_recipe: Extract the zingiber officinale rhizome by peeling and grinding it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Add the zingiber officinale powder to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken for about 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and let it set at room temperature for 1 hour before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams helps with altitude sickness because of its ability to adapt to high altitudes and improve oxygenation. The cream's active compound, ginsenoside, increases blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, reducing fatigue and shortness of breath. Additionally, Panax ginseng's antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress caused by high-altitude exposure, promoting overall well-being and reducing the severity of altitude sickness symptoms. This natural remedy supports the body's natural adaptation to high altitudes.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: Help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the body.
Saponins: Assist in mitigating altitude sickness by enhancing cardiovascular function, improving oxygen delivery to tissues, and reducing oxidative stress.
Phenolic acids: Contribute to alleviating altitude sickness by exerting antioxidant effects, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative damage in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Panax ginseng contain ginsenosides, which help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms such as fatigue and shortness of breath.
Barks: The barks of Panax ginseng contain antioxidants and ginsenosides, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with high altitudes.
Leaves: The leaves of Panax ginseng contain antioxidants and may help protect against cell damage caused by high altitude exposure.
remedy_recipe: Gather 9g of dried panax ginseng root and 100ml of base cream with a ph level between 5.5 and 6.5.
Infuse the dried ginseng root in 50ml of distilled water at 70-80°c for 30 minutes then steep for 1 hour.
Strain the ginseng infusion and combine it with 50ml of base cream and 1.5g of emulsifying wax.
Heat the mixture gently at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes while mixing until it forms a smooth and even consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-60 minutes before applying it to the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain adaptogenic properties that aid in stress relief and resilience. At high altitudes, the body's stress response is triggered due to low oxygen levels, leading to symptoms like headaches and fatigue. Rhodiola rosea's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to these changes, promoting a balanced response to stress. This can help alleviate symptoms associated with altitude sickness, such as altitude-induced fatigue and decreased cognitive function. It may also enhance physical and mental performance at high elevations.
remedy_constituents: Rosavin: It helps alleviate altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
Salidroside: This constituent helps mitigate the effects of high altitude by improving blood flow and reducing fatigue.
Tyrosol: It helps mitigate altitude sickness by reducing the production of stress hormones like cortisol and adrenaline.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of active compounds like rosavin and salidroside, which are believed to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.

Leaves: The leaves are used in some formulations due to their rosmarinic acid content, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help mitigate the effects of altitude sickness.
Stems: The stems are occasionally used in Rhodiola rosea creams for altitude sickness due to their moderate levels of active compounds, although they are less potent than the roots or leaves.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of rhodiola rosea root powder and mix it with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and blend the rhodiola rosea evenly.
Add 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of shea butter to the mixture and stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 15 minutes before whipping it with a hand mixer.
Pour the mixture into small containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to set the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain quinine, a natural compound derived from the Cinchona tree. Quinine has been shown to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as dizziness and nausea, by improving circulation and oxygen delivery to the body's cells. The antimalarial properties of quinine also help to counteract the effects of high altitude on the body, making Cinchona officinalis creams a popular remedy among travelers and adventurers.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine helps to reduce the severity of altitude sickness by inhibiting the accumulation of lactic acid in the body, which occurs due to hypoxia at high altitudes.
Quinidine: Quinidine is a vasodilator that helps to reduce blood pressure, thereby improving blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, which are often affected by altitude sickness.
Caffeine: Caffeine is a vasodilator that helps to increase blood flow to the lungs and brain, improving oxygen delivery to these organs and reducing the severity of altitude sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary part used due to their high concentration of quinine, a key compound in treating altitude sickness.
Barks: The barks are also used as they contain quinine and other alkaloids that help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
(optional) Rhyzomes: Some sources may use the rhyzomes, which contain a lower concentration of quinine compared to the leaves and barks, but still offer some therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried cinchona officinalis bark and 60g of vegetable oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean container.
Mix 15g of beeswax and 30g of cocoa butter in a double boiler to melt at 160-180°f for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted wax and butter mixture with the cinchona officinalis bark and sweet almond oil in a blender.
Blend the mixture on high speed for 2 minutes to create a smooth and consistent texture with 5% yield.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain compounds that stimulate the immune system and promote natural healing. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea help to reduce swelling and pain associated with altitude sickness. Additionally, it has been shown to increase oxygenation and improve respiratory function, allowing the body to adapt to high elevations more efficiently. This natural remedy can aid in alleviating symptoms such as headaches and fatigue, making it a valuable tool for travelers and athletes alike.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: They help with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can be triggered by high altitude conditions.
Phenolic Acids: They help with altitude sickness by exhibiting antioxidant properties, protecting the body from damage caused by free radicals at high altitudes.
Cichoric Acid: They help with altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can help the body adapt to the lower oxygen levels at high altitudes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Echinacea purpurea flowers are the most commonly used part in creams for altitude sickness due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms.

Roots: Echinacea purpurea roots are used in creams for altitude sickness because they contain compounds like polysaccharides and glycoproteins that stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation.

Leaves: Echinacea purpurea leaves are used in creams for altitude sickness due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help combat oxidative stress caused by high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in the morning after the dew has dried for medicinal use.
Dry the harvested flowers and roots in a single layer at 40°c for 2 days to remove moisture.
Combine 10 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and 5 grams of dried roots in a mortar.
Mix the dried plant material with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes then pour it into a container for cooling and hardening.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue commonly associated with high altitudes. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow may also aid in the body's adaptation to lower oxygen levels, making it easier to breathe and increasing overall comfort. This natural remedy can provide relief and support for individuals ascending to high altitudes.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides (Glycyrrhizin): They help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can be triggered by high altitude.

Saponins: These compounds help by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and protecting against cellular damage caused by high altitude, thereby alleviating symptoms like headaches and fatigue.
Flavonoids (Isoflavones, Phenolic acids): They help by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to altitude sickness symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are commonly used to make creams for various purposes, including skin care and soothing irritated skin, as they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: They are also used to make creams for relieving stress and anxiety, as they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the body.
Roots: These are used to make creams for reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as they contain compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 5 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root in 100ml of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to discard solids.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler to melt at 60 degrees celsius.
Gradually add 50ml of the infused oil mixture to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture to combine.
Pour the resulting mixture into a sterilized container and allow to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain adaptogenic properties that aid the body in acclimatizing to high elevations. The Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, promotes oxygenation and circulation, which are crucial for individuals experiencing altitude sickness. By reducing stress and inflammation, these creams help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue. Additionally, they may enhance the body's natural ability to adapt to low oxygen levels, making them a potentially beneficial remedy for individuals traveling to high-altitude destinations.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with high-altitude conditions, contributing to altitude sickness.

Alkaloids (withanine and withaferin A): These alkaloids exhibit adaptogenic properties, helping the body to adapt to high-altitude conditions, reduce stress, and improve overall health, potentially mitigating altitude sickness symptoms.

Saponins: Saponins in Withania somnifera may help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, all of which can contribute to alleviating the symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are used to make creams for altitude sickness because they contain withanolides, which help to mitigate the effects of high altitude.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for altitude sickness due to their adaptogenic properties, which aid in acclimatization and reduce symptoms associated with high altitude.
Barks: The barks are used to make creams for altitude sickness because they contain withanolides and other compounds that help to improve circulation and reduce fatigue at high elevations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of withania somnifera roots in the morning after the dew has dried for optimal quality.
Dry the roots in a warm place for 7 days at 35-40°c or use a dehydrator to speed up the process.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or coffee grinder.
Mix 20 grams of the powdered root with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted and a smooth cream has formed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica creams helps with altitude sickness because they contain ephedrine alkaloids, which stimulate the respiratory system to increase oxygen intake. This helps the body adapt to higher elevations more efficiently. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and discomfort caused by altitude sickness. The increased circulation and oxygenation provided by Ephedra sinica creams also aid in reducing fatigue and shortness of breath, making it easier to acclimatize to high altitudes and enjoy outdoor activities in mountainous regions.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to alleviate respiratory difficulties caused by high altitude, such as shortness of breath and coughing.
Caffeine: Works as a vasodilator, improving blood flow to the lungs and brain, which may help reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Catechin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body caused by high altitude, potentially alleviating symptoms such as muscle pain and nausea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make creams due to their high concentration of ephedrine, a compound that helps to reduce altitude sickness symptoms by increasing oxygen supply to the body.
Stems: They are used in creams to take advantage of their ephedrine content, which can help to alleviate altitude sickness by improving circulation and reducing shortness of breath.

Seeds: They are used in creams due to their high content of ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, compounds that can help to alleviate altitude sickness symptoms by increasing oxygen supply and reducing respiratory distress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried ephedra sinica root at 8 am on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested ephedra sinica root at 30 degrees celsius for 3 days.
Infuse 20 grams of dried ephedra sinica root in 200 milliliters of hot water for 20 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine the infused ephedra sinica liquid with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture to create a smooth cream.
